Tool Call: retrieve("Rotor syndrome symptoms and pathophysiology")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the detailed evidence gathered, Rotor syndrome indeed fits the description perfectly. With key characteristics being prominent conjugated hyperbilirubinemia (>50%), normal liver function tests apart from mildly raised transaminases, and no signs of obstructive jaundice or other secondary causes, all points strongly favor Rotor syndrome over other options listed. Moreover, the lack of pigmented liver and absence of other comorbidities make it distinct even from Dubin-Johnson syndrome. Therefore, my revised stance firmly supports choosing \boxed{A}. The evidence reinforces the accuracy of initial reasoning applied previously.
